content.text: 30 ссылок. Запущу параллельную верификацию — разобью на группы.

content.name: Agent
content.input:
[object Object]{"description": "Verify references batch 1 (10)", "prompt": "Verify these 10 academic references. For each one, search the web (Google Scholar, arXiv, Semantic Scholar) and confirm: (1) the paper exists, (2) the title is correct, (3) the authors are correct and complete (for entries with "et al." or "and others", that's fine — just verify the listed ones), (4) the venue and year are correct. Report any discrepancies.

1. Azaria, Amos and Mitchell, Tom. "The Internal State of an LLM Knows When It's Lying." Findings of EMNLP, 2023.

2. Bürger, Lennart and Hamprecht, Fred A. and Nadler, Boaz. "Truth is Universal: Robust Detection of Lies in LLMs." NeurIPS, 2024.

3. Burns, Collin and Ye, Haotian and Klein, Dan and Steinhardt, Jacob. "Discovering Latent Knowledge in Language Models Without Supervision." ICLR, 2023.

4. Chlon, Leon and Karim, Ahmed and Chlon, Maggie and Awada, MarcAntonio. "Order Sensitivity and Information Budgeting: Understanding Why Language Models Hallucinate." arXiv:2509.11208, 2025.

5. Deletang, Gregoire and Ruoss, Anian and Grau-Moya, Jordi et al. "Language Modeling Is Compression." ICLR, 2024.

6. Elazar, Yanai and Kassner, Nora and Ravfogel, Shauli and Ravichander, Abhilasha and Hovy, Eduard and Schütze, Hinrich and Goldberg, Yoav. "Measuring Causal Effects of Data Statistics on Language Model's Factual Predictions." arXiv:2207.14251, 2022.

7. Penedo, Guilherme and Kydlíček, Hynek and Lozhkov, Anton and Mitchell, Margaret and Wolf, Thomas et al. "The FineWeb Datasets: Decanting the Web for the Finest Text Data at Scale." arXiv:2406.17557, 2024.

8. Goldblum, Micah and Finzi, Marc and Rowan, Keefer and Wilson, Andrew Gordon. "The No Free Lunch Theorem, Kolmogorov Complexity, and the Role of Inductive Biases in Machine Learning." ICML, 2024.

9. Grünwald, Peter D. "The Minimum Description Length Principle." MIT Press, 2007.

10. Gurnee, Wes and Tegmark, Max. "Language Models Represent Space and Time." ICLR, 2024.

Be concise — just list discrepancies or "OK" for each.", "subagent_type": "general-purpose"}

content.name: Agent
content.input:
[object Object]{"description": "Verify references batch 2 (10)", "prompt": "Verify these 10 academic references. For each one, search the web (Google Scholar, arXiv, Semantic Scholar) and confirm: (1) the paper exists, (2) the title is correct, (3) the authors listed are correct, (4) the venue and year are correct. Report any discrepancies.

1. Huang, Yuzhen and Zhang, Jinghan and Shan, Zifei and He, Junxian. "Compression Represents Intelligence Linearly." COLM, 2024.

2. Joshi, Nitarshan and Rando, Javier and Saparov, Abulhair and Kim, Najoung and He, He. "Personas as a Way to Model Truthfulness in Language Models." EMNLP, 2024.

3. Kadavath, Saurav and Conerly, Tom and Askell, Amanda et al. "Language Models (Mostly) Know What They Know." arXiv:2207.05221, 2022.

4. Kalai, Adam Tauman and Vempala, Santosh S. "Calibrated Language Models Must Hallucinate." STOC, 2024.

5. Kandpal, Nikhil and Deng, Haikang and Roberts, Adam and Wallace, Eric and Raffel, Colin. "Large Language Models Struggle to Learn Long-Tail Knowledge." ICML, 2023.

6. Longpre, Shayne and Perisetla, Kartik and Chen, Anthony and Ramesh, Nikhil and DuBois, Chris and Singh, Sameer. "Entity-Based Knowledge Conflicts in Question Answering." EMNLP, 2021.

7. Li, Jiahuan and Cao, Yiqing and Huang, Shujian and Chen, Jiajun. "Formality is Favored: Unraveling the Learning Preferences of Large Language Models on Data with Conflicting Knowledge." EMNLP, 2024.

8. Li, Kenneth and Hopkins, Aspen K. and Bau, David and Viegas, Fernanda and Pfister, Hanspeter and Wattenberg, Martin. "Emergent World Representations: Exploring a Sequence Model Trained on a Synthetic Task." ICLR, 2023.

9. Li, Kenneth and Patel, Oam and Viegas, Fernanda and Pfister, Hanspeter and Wattenberg, Martin. "Inference-Time Intervention: Eliciting Truthful Answers from a Language Model." NeurIPS, 2023.

10. Liu, Ziming and Zhong, Ziqian and Tegmark, Max. "Grokking as Compression: A Nonlinear Complexity Perspective." arXiv:2310.05918, 2023.

Be concise — just list discrepancies or "OK" for each.", "subagent_type": "general-purpose"}

content.name: Agent
content.input:
[object Object]{"description": "Verify references batch 3 (10)", "prompt": "Verify these 10 academic references. For each one, search the web (Google Scholar, arXiv, Semantic Scholar) and confirm: (1) the paper exists, (2) the title is correct, (3) the authors listed are correct, (4) the venue and year are correct. Report any discrepancies.

1. Marty, Tom and Elmoznino, Eric and Gagnon, Leo and Kasetty, Tejas and Nishikawa-Toomey, Mizu and Mittal, Sarthak and Lajoie, Guillaume and Sridhar, Dhanya. "A Compression Perspective on Simplicity Bias." arXiv:2603.25839, 2026.

2. Aksenov, Vitaly and Bodnia, Eve and Freedman, Michael H. and Mulligan, Michael. "Compression is All You Need: Modeling Mathematics." arXiv:2603.20396, 2026.

3. Marks, Samuel and Tegmark, Max. "The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in Large Language Model Representations of True/False Datasets." COLM, 2024.

4. McGraw, Kenneth O. and Wong, S. P. "A Common Language Effect Size Statistic." Psychological Bulletin, 111(2), 361-365, 1992.

5. Mingard, Chris and Valle-Perez, Guillermo and Sherrington, David and Louis, Ard A. "Is SGD a Bayesian Sampler? Well, Almost." JMLR, 2021.

6. Nanda, Neel and Chan, Lawrence and Lieberum, Tom and Smith, Jess and Steinhardt, Jacob. "Progress Measures for Grokking via Mechanistic Interpretability." ICLR, 2023.

7. Pan, Zhixuan and Wang, Shaowen and Li, Jian. "Understanding LLM Behaviors via Compression: Data Generation, Knowledge Acquisition and Scaling Laws." arXiv:2504.09597, 2025.

8. Ravfogel, Shauli and Yehudai, Gal and Linzen, Tal and Bietti, Alberto and Bruna, Joan. "Emergence of Linear Truth Encodings in Language Models." NeurIPS, 2025.

9. Wan, Jun and Mei, Lingrui. "Large Language Models as Computable Approximations to Solomonoff Induction." arXiv:2505.15784, 2025.

10. Xie, Jian and Zhang, Kai and Chen, Jiangjie and Lou, Renze and Su, Yu. "Adaptive Chameleon or Stubborn Sloth: Revealing the Behavior of Large Language Models in Knowledge Conflicts." ICLR, 2024.

Also verify these classic references:
11. Popper, Karl. "The Logic of Scientific Discovery." Hutchinson, 1959.
12. Rissanen, Jorma. "Modeling by Shortest Data Description." Automatica, 14(5), 465-471, 1978.
13. Shannon, Claude E. "A Mathematical Theory of Communication." Bell System Technical Journal, 27(3), 379-423, 1948.
14. Solomonoff, Ray J. "A Formal Theory of Inductive Inference." Information and Control, 7(1), 1-22, 1964.
15. Valle-Perez, Guillermo and Camargo, Chico Q. and Louis, Ard A. "Deep Learning Generalizes Because the Parameter-Function Map Is Biased Towards Simple Functions." ICLR, 2019.
16. Rolnick, David and Veit, Andreas and Belongie, Serge and Shavit, Nir. "Deep Learning is Robust to Massive Label Noise." arXiv:1705.10694, 2017.
17. Zhang, Chiyuan and Bengio, Samy and Hardt, Moritz and Recht, Benjamin and Vinyals, Oriol. "Understanding Deep Learning Requires Rethinking Generalization." ICLR, 2017.

Be concise — just list discrepancies or "OK" for each.", "subagent_type": "general-purpose"}
